overview: This Legacy Black/White deck operates as a tempo-control hybrid, leveraging early interaction to stabilize against aggressive strategies. It transitions into a mid-game setup phase using Stoneforge Mystic to fetch protective artifacts, then stabilizes with Solitude for sustained card advantage. The primary win condition is Yorion, Sky Nomad, which grows into an unblockable threat while Solitude continuously refills your hand.

keep_criteria: Hands with two lands and early interaction
Hands containing Yorion or Solitude with removal
Hands with Stoneforge Mystic and artifact slots
Keep 4+ land hands with a curve of 1-2 mana spells (Solitude, Noble Hierarch, Birds of Paradise, Thoughtseize).
Keep 5+ land hands with a turn-1 play (Thalia, Tarmogoyf, Scavenging Ooze) or a turn-2 play (Yorion, Stoneforge Mystic).
Prioritize hands that can deploy a tax effect or card draw by turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Heavy land counts without early plays
Hands lacking removal or card advantage
Mulligan pure land, all removal, or hands missing basics if the format demands them.

early_game: Play lands efficiently and hold interaction for key threats
Use removal to clear the path for early tempo plays
Prioritize survival over early artifact deployment
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Noble Hierarch or Birds of Paradise for mana acceleration, or Thoughtseize/Duress to disrupt.
If opening Stoneforge Mystic, play land, cast SFM, fetch land, search for Batterskull or Sword of Feast and Famine.
Turn 2: Deploy Thalia to tax opponent's non-creature spells, or cast Yorion to stabilize.
Sequence lands to ensure colored mana for Solitude/Thoughtseize by turn 2.
mid_game: Activate Stoneforge Mystic to fetch protective boots or greaves
Cast Solitude once you have a steady stream of cards to exile
Deploy Yorion when the board is clear or protected
Preserve Solitude for key threats (counters, finishers, combo pieces).
Use Thoughtseize/Duress to disrupt opponent's hand before committing threats.
Manage the graveyard with Rest in Peace/Surgical Extraction to prevent recursion.
Avoid overcommitting to the board if the opponent has sweepers or counterspells.
Commit threats when you have removal ready or when the opponent is tapped out.
Hold back if they have potential sweepers or counters.
late_game: Use Solitude to maintain hand size and outvalue opponents
Push damage with Yorion while cycling through your deck
Leverage artifact synergies to protect your win condition
Yorion, Sky Nomad (body + flying + lifelink), Batterskull (on a resilient threat or Yorion), Sword of Feast and Famine (for card advantage and removal).
Field of the Dead can close games if the graveyard is clear and you have enough 1/1 tokens.
Go all-in when you have established board control, the opponent has no sweepers, or you can trade Yorion into a key threat.
Commit when you can protect your finisher with removal or tax effects, and when you have card advantage to sustain the push.

key_cards.name: Yorion, Sky Nomad
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and tempo threat
key_cards.notes: Play when the board is clear; use its flying and +1/+1 counter generation to pressure opponents while they lack answers.

key_cards.name: Solitude
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have multiple cards in hand; exile cards to draw new ones, maintaining hand size and finding answers.

key_cards.name: Stoneforge Mystic
key_cards.notes: Use to fetch protective artifacts like boots or greaves; activate before playing the artifact to ensure it enters untapped and protected.

sequencing_tips: Hold removal until you see the opponent's key threats
Play Solitude after you have established a steady card flow
Activate Stoneforge Mystic before casting the fetched artifact to avoid counterspells
Deploy Yorion only when you have protection or the board is empty
Yorion is both a win condition and a stabilizer
Solitude is your most powerful tool—save it for game-ending threats or key combo pieces.
Stoneforge Mystic finds Batterskull for a massive body or Sword of Feast and Famine for card advantage and removal.
Rest in Peace is often a game-winning sideboard card.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early removal and play Yorion as soon as possible to race
Use Solitude to outvalue their hand size and push damage with Yorion
Disrupt their setup with interaction and use Solitude to exile key pieces

Use Solitude to remove counterspellers. Cast threats in response to counterspells if possible. Use Thoughtseize to remove key counters. Do not overcommit to a single threat; apply incremental pressure and force them to use multiple answers.

Apply early pressure to disrupt their mana development. Use Solitude on key ramp creatures. Deploy Thalia to tax their non-creature spells. Graveyard hate to stop recursion. Force them to spend resources on interaction rather than developing their board.

Win the resource war. Prioritize Thoughtseize/Duress to disrupt their hand. Use Solitude to remove key threats. Deploy Thalia to tax their non-creature spells. Win the graveyard war with Rest in Peace/Surgical Extraction. Control the board with tax effects and efficient trades.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Supreme Verdict, Anger of the Gods), counters (Force of Will, Counterspell), combo pieces (Inverter of Truth, Storm targets), and graveyard recursion.
Note when the opponent taps out for a big spell or when they start casting non-basic lands for ramp.
Track their hand size after Thoughtseize/Duress to predict their next move.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype: Death and Taxes (Yorion) - Black/White, Format: legacy, Signature cards: Yorion, Sky Nomad, Solitude, Stoneforge Mystic

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards: Yorion, Sky Nomad, Solitude, Stoneforge Mystic

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards: Yorion, Sky Nomad, Solitude, Stoneforge Mystic
